text: Plumb must be installable via both pip and uv using the package name 'plumb-dev'
The CLI command must be 'plumb'
Tests must support comment-based markers using '# plumb:req-XXXXXXXX' format for linking to requirements
Tests must support function name-based linking using 'test_req_XXXXXXXX_' format for linking to requirements
Both linking formats must be supported for backwards compatibility
The system must filter for spec-relevant content during decision extraction in both conversation analysis and diff analysis methods
The system must implement semantic similarity checking to handle duplicate or similar decisions effectively
Classification tasks must not generate reasoning traces to maintain optimal performance
LLM-based deduplication must utilize Claude Haiku 4.5 as the designated model
The deduplicate_decisions() function must accept a use_llm parameter that defaults to False
The system must support loading environment variables from .env files to manage configuration settings
The plumb init command must create a .env file in the project directory
The system must support ANTHROPIC_API_KEY configuration through both environment variables and .env files
Tests must be linked to requirements through requirement ID comments to establish traceability between test functions and specific requirements
All tests must be organized in the tests/ directory with specific test files for each component
Tests without requirement links must be treated as sync violations that indicate either missing requirements or unnecessary tests
The system must support file ignore patterns through a '.plumbignore' file
The system must provide a '--all' command option for the 'plumb approve' command to approve all changes at once
Generated cache and coverage files must be excluded from commits
The system must include a 'check' command as an alias for manual decision scanning
Plumb must handle conversations that span multiple Claude Code session files by reading and merging them chronologically
The sync command must provide progress indicators with status updates to give users feedback during operation
The workflow must require an explicit sync step after approving decisions
The system must stage sync output before re-committing changes
The system must use a branch-sharded structure for decision logs
The system must provide CLI commands for merging decision logs from different branches
Users must be able to migrate existing decision logs from the old monolithic format to the new branch-sharded layout through dedicated CLI commands
The plumb merge-decisions <branch> command must allow users to merge decisions from feature branches back to main
A find_decision_branch() function must help locate which branch file contains a specific decision ID
The core LLM integration must be designed with a WholeFileSpecUpdater that takes full spec content and decisions as input
The WholeFileSpecUpdater must output section_updates for existing sections and new_sections for brand new sections
The approach must accept rewriting whole sections instead of making surgical edits for markdown specs
An OutlineMerger component must handle structural changes when new sections need to be added or content needs reordering
The system must include helper functions for DuckDB result type conversion
The system must use _clean_duckdb_row() and _to_python_native() functions to convert DuckDB result types to Python native types for Pydantic compatibility
The system must implement comprehensive test coverage for migration and merge functionality, including edge cases and error conditions
Plumb must solve a bounded problem and be holdable in a single programmer's head
All LLM-powered functions must be implemented as DSPy programs, not open-ended agents
Plumb must use the Anthropic Claude SDK via the user's existing account as its inference provider
Claude Sonnet 4.6 must serve as the default model for all programs
Plumb must operate as a git hook and CLI tool without changing how the user writes code
The pre-commit hook must ensure that every committed state has been reviewed and approved
A commit must represent a fully reconciled snapshot of spec, tests, and code
Plumb must use Claude Code session data when available
When Claude Code session data is not available, Plumb must fall back to diff-only analysis
The system must prevent duplicate decisions through a multi-stage pipeline using exact matching, Jaccard similarity, and LLM semantic analysis
Only prescriptive choices that affect system design, behavior, architecture, or data models must be captured as decisions
Process observations, tooling choices, and diagnostic findings must be filtered out
Spec updates must use a search-and-replace approach with edits containing old_text/new_text pairs rather than full section replacement
Section updates must use exact header matching first, then fall back to normalized matching for minor variations in header formatting
The system must integrate with git hooks to automatically trigger workflows based on repository events
DSPy programs must support program-specific model configuration through a model configuration array in config.json
Pattern parsing logic must be modularized into separate functions for reusability across programs
The system must include an extract_outline function that extracts markdown headers from content
The programs module must include token estimation, chunking, and concurrent mapper functionality to handle large datasets
The system must use chunked mapping with ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent processing
The system must support different merge strategies for result combination
Plumb must store all state in a .plumb/ folder at the root of the user's repository
The .plumb/ folder must be committed to version control
The system must track which requirements have been modified and only send dirty requirements to the mapper for processing
The hook must validate API access before proceeding with analysis
If API authentication fails, the hook must exit non-zero and block the commit
The hook must analyze the staged diff and the Claude Code conversation log using the unified conversation reading system
The system must auto-detect Claude Code sessions vs legacy logs
The system must read from Claude Code's actual session files at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/<uuid>.jsonl
The hook must write pending decisions to branch-specific decision log files with filesystem-safe path sanitization
The hook must set the last_extracted_at timestamp
The hook must print a machine-readable JSON summary of pending decisions to stdout and exit non-zero, aborting the commit
Claude Code's skill must read that output and begin presenting decisions to the user using AskUserQuestion format, one at a time
The skill must call the appropriate per-decision command based on user response
The system must include explicit safeguards to never approve, reject, or edit decisions on the user's behalf
Decision operations must support branch-specific handling through branch parameter support
For rejected decisions, the skill must invoke plumb modify <id>
Once all decisions are resolved, the skill must re-run git commit
The commit must only land when there are zero pending decisions
The system must draft commit messages after decision review and include the list of approved decisions
When API authentication fails, the system must raise a custom PlumbAuthError exception
The PlumbAuthError must alert the user and provide clear instructions to set their API key
API key validation must be implemented as a separate validate_api_access function
The validate_api_access function must be called before all LLM operations
All authentication functionality must have comprehensive test coverage
Existing tests must be updated to mock API validation calls to ensure test suites remain unaffected
The system must use gitignore-style patterns for ignore functionality
Default patterns must be used when no .plumbignore file exists
After commit completion, the post-commit hook must clear the last_extracted_at timestamp
The conversation parser must handle Claude Code's type/message schema format
The conversation parser must convert tool usage blocks to text format '[tool: Name] description' for consistency
The system must include comprehensive documentation for the Plumb skill
The system must implement intelligent deduplication by first running Jaccard similarity filtering
LLM deduplication must be applied as a second pass when 2 or more candidates remain
The system must use DSPy's context manager with Haiku LM for deduplication while preserving Sonnet for other operations
The deduplication process must prioritize approved and synced decisions over recent ones in the comparison set
The system must use an expanded context window of 200 decisions instead of 50 for more comprehensive duplicate detection
The system must generate complete and runnable tests rather than stub files with TODO comments
Test generation must use increased context limits to accommodate complete test code
The test generator must produce fully functional test code that can be executed immediately
The test generator must only run if tests don't already exist
The system must provide read_all_decisions() as the primary API function for accessing decisions across all branches
The review command must pre-compute decision branches to optimize performance and ensure correct shard targeting for updates
Users must be able to migrate from the monolithic decision file to the sharded layout using the plumb migrate command
The system must implement spec updates using a single LLM call per spec file
The output schema must contain section_updates and new_sections
The system must make a second call only when new_sections is non-empty
Structural reasoning for section placement must be kept separate from content generation
The system must keep edit operations simple without complex operation sets
The system must accept the risk of unintended edits in exchange for performance gains while mitigating through clear prompting
The legacy decisions path function must be maintained for migration detection purposes
The hook must analyze the staged diff and read Claude Code's native session files directly from auto-detected paths
The system must extract prescriptive choices while excluding observations and diagnostics
Within-batch similarity deduplication must prevent duplicate decisions in the same batch
Time awareness must use both last_commit datetime and last_extracted_at timestamp as cutoffs
The system must take the later of the two timestamps to determine which conversations to process
The hook must write pending decisions to decisions.jsonl, passing current decisions and recent decisions from the last couple commits to the deduplication pass
The system must use exact deduplication and LLM-based semantic deduplication
Source summaries must be structured as per-file mappings to enable granular tracking
All documentation files including SKILL files and CLAUDE.md must be kept consistent with the same sync workflow requirements
plumb init must check that the current directory is a git repository and exit with an error if not
plumb init must create the .plumb/ directory if it does not exist
plumb init must prompt the user interactively to provide a path to a spec file or directory of spec markdown files
plumb init must validate that the spec path exists and contains .md files
plumb init must use recursive search (rglob) to find markdown files within directories and suggest discovered spec files
plumb init must prompt the user to provide a path to a test file or test directory and validate that the path exists
plumb init must scan repository for test directories and files to provide suggestions
plumb init must write .plumb/config.json with the provided paths
plumb init must create a .plumbignore file in the project root if it does not exist
plumb init must install the git pre-commit hook by writing a script to .git/hooks/pre-commit that calls plumb hook
plumb init must set the pre-commit hook script as executable
plumb init must install the Claude Code skill locally by copying plumb/skill/SKILL.md to .claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md in the project root
plumb init must create .claude/skills/plumb/ directories if they do not exist
plumb init must only perform project-local installation and never write to the user's global ~/.claude/ directory
plumb init must append a Plumb status block to CLAUDE.md at the project root
plumb init must create CLAUDE.md if it does not exist
plumb init must run plumb parse-spec to do an initial parse of the spec into requirements
plumb init must print a confirmation summary to the terminal, including confirmation that the skill was installed at .claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md
plumb hook must read .plumb/config.json and exit 0 silently if not found
plumb hook must get the current staged diff via git diff --cached
plumb hook must get the current branch name
plumb hook must detect amends by comparing the HEAD commit's parent SHA to last_commit
plumb hook must delete decisions in decisions.jsonl where commit_sha == last_commit before re-running analysis when amend is detected
plumb hook must detect broken references by checking all SHAs in decisions.jsonl against git history
plumb hook must flag unreachable SHAs with 'ref_status': 'broken' and include a warning in output
plumb hook must run the Diff Analysis DSPy program on the staged diff
plumb hook must attempt to locate and read the Claude Code conversation log
If conversation log is found, plumb hook must read and chunk turns since last_commit timestamp
plumb hook must use unified conversation reading interface to support multiple sessions
plumb hook must merge conversation turns from all relevant session files modified after the last commit and sort chronologically
plumb hook must handle multi-line assistant responses that span multiple JSONL lines
plumb hook must convert tool_use blocks to '[tool: Name] description' format in conversation turns
plumb hook must run Decision Extraction per chunk when conversation log is found
If conversation log is not found, plumb hook must skip conversation analysis and note 'conversation_available': false in each decision object
plumb hook must filter out decisions marked as non-spec-relevant during extraction before creating Decision records
plumb hook must merge and deduplicate decisions across chunks using DuckDB for cross-shard queries
Deduplication must check against all existing decisions (both pending and resolved) instead of only resolved ones
plumb hook must implement within-batch similarity deduplication to compare new decisions against each other using Jaccard similarity check
For each decision with no associated question, plumb hook must run Question Synthesizer with program-specific model configuration
plumb hook must write all new decisions with status: 'pending' to decisions.jsonl
plumb hook must implement decisions sharding using a TDD approach with core refactor, DuckDB integration, caller updates, new commands, and comprehensive testing coverage
plumb hook must run plumb parse-spec to update requirements cache for any modified spec files
plumb hook must batch spec updates across all decisions instead of running spec update for each individual decision
plumb hook must use search-and-replace pairs pattern for spec updates
LLM must return list of {old_text, new_text} operations instead of rewriting entire sections
plumb hook must implement a two-tier approach for spec updates: Tier 1 for existing section updates and Tier 2 for structural changes
For structural changes, plumb hook must use outline-first approach where LLM returns updated header outline first, then fills in content for new sections
plumb hook must create OutlineMerger signature for conditional second call that takes current outline and new headers, returns merged outline with proper positioning
If pending decisions exist, plumb hook must check whether it is running in a TTY or as a subprocess
In TTY mode, plumb hook must print a human-readable summary of pending decisions with instructions to run plumb review
In non-TTY mode, plumb hook must print a machine-readable JSON object to stdout
plumb hook must exit non-zero in both cases when pending decisions exist, aborting the commit
If no pending decisions exist, plumb hook must run plumb coverage, print a brief summary, update last_commit and last_commit_branch in config.json, and exit 0
The hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
If Plumb itself fails, it must print a warning to stderr and exit 0 so the commit is not blocked
plumb hook --dry-run must run the full hook analysis on staged changes but not write to decisions.jsonl and always exit 0
plumb diff must read staged changes via git diff --cached
plumb diff must run Diff Analysis on the staged diff
plumb diff must read and chunk the conversation log (turns since last_commit) if available
plumb diff must run Decision Extraction per chunk
plumb diff must print a preview to the terminal including summary of staged code changes, estimated number of decisions, and brief description of each estimated decision
plumb diff must make no writes to .plumb/
plumb review must read .plumb/decisions.jsonl, filtering for status == 'pending'
plumb review must accept an optional --branch <name> flag to filter by branch
When multiple session files exist, plumb review must use the most recent session file by modification time or match the current session ID for session selection
If no pending decisions exist, plumb review must print 'No pending decisions.' and exit 0
For each pending decision, plumb review must display the framing question, decision made, branch, file and line references, ref_status, and most related current spec text
plumb review must prompt the user with options: [a]pprove, [r]eject, [e]dit, [s]kip
For reject option, plumb review must prompt for reason and optionally run plumb modify <id> automatically
For edit option, plumb review must allow user to provide replacement decision text
After all decisions are resolved, plumb review must run plumb sync for all approved/edited decisions
Decision classification must default spec_relevant field to True to ensure decisions pass through for user review when LLM classification is uncertain
plumb approve <id> must approve a single decision by ID and update its status to 'approved' in decisions.jsonl
plumb approve <id> must then run plumb sync for that decision only
The status display must include a visual bar chart representation for coverage statistics and use Rich's Status spinner that updates in-place for progress indication
plumb reject <id> [--reason '<text>'] must reject a single decision by ID and update its status to 'rejected' and record the reason
plumb reject must not modify code or spec
plumb edit <id> '<new decision text>' must replace the decision text for a given decision ID with the user-provided text
plumb edit must update status to 'edited' and then run plumb sync for that decision only
plumb modify <id> must modify the staged code to satisfy a rejected decision
plumb modify must read the decision object for <id> from decisions.jsonl and verify status == 'rejected'
plumb modify must read the staged diff
plumb modify must call the Claude API with the staged diff, decision made, rejection reason, current spec, and instruction to modify staged code
plumb modify must apply the proposed modification to the staged files
plumb modify must run pytest on the test suite to verify that tests still pass after modification
If tests pass, plumb modify must print the resulting diff, stage the modified files, and update decision status to 'rejected_modified'
If tests fail, plumb modify must print the failure output, not stage the modification, prompt user to resolve manually, and update decision status to 'rejected_manual'
In non-TTY mode, plumb modify must return a machine-readable JSON result
plumb must never commit the modification and only stage it
plumb sync must add early exit check to avoid expensive operations when no unsynced decisions exist
plumb sync must read decisions from decisions.jsonl with status 'approved' or 'edited' that have not yet been synced
For each spec file, plumb sync must run WholeFileSpecUpdater processing all decisions affecting the spec file together
WholeFileSpecUpdater must return section_updates for existing sections and new_sections for brand new sections
If new_sections is non-empty, plumb sync must run OutlineMerger to determine proper positioning of new sections within the spec structure
plumb sync must write the updated spec file to disk using temp file → rename
plumb sync must fix newline formatting by ensuring proper newlines between section headers and body content
plumb sync must run Test Generator to generate organized tests for requirements not covered by existing tests
Tests must be organized by functionality with proper requirement links in the format test_<req_id>_<description>
The generator must read only source files referenced by file_refs on synced decisions for context
plumb sync must write generated tests to test_generated.py using temp file → rename
plumb sync must run plumb parse-spec to re-cache requirements
plumb sync must set synced_at on each processed decision
plumb sync must print a summary of spec sections updated and test files created
plumb sync must use .plumbignore file with default patterns for documentation, build files, and IDE configurations to exclude irrelevant files from processing
plumb parse-spec must read all markdown files in spec_paths from config.json
For each file or paragraph block, plumb parse-spec must run Requirement Parser to produce explicit, testable requirement statements
plumb parse-spec must assign each requirement a stable ID based on a hash of its content
plumb parse-spec must write results to .plumb/requirements.json
Requirements with matching hashes must not be re-processed, preserving existing metadata including created_at and last_seen_commit timestamps
plumb parse-spec must use regex patterns PLUMB_MARKER_RE and FUNC_NAME_RE to extract requirement IDs from test files for coverage mapping
plumb parse-spec must process all requirements and AST summaries of every non-test .py file through the CodeCoverageMapper using chunked mapping approach
plumb parse-spec must use fan-out chunking to group source files under token budget (default 60000 tokens) and broadcast requirements to each chunk
plumb parse-spec must implement granular per-file hashing with structured v2 cache format that tracks individual requirement results for efficient cache invalidation
plumb parse-spec must preserve existing incremental caching logic by applying chunking inside the LLM call after dirty requirements are determined
plumb parse-spec must extract source file paths from evidence strings using pattern matching against known files to determine requirement-to-file mappings
plumb parse-spec must use greedy packing to group source/test summaries into chunks and ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent chunk processing
plumb parse-spec must merge results from multiple chunks using custom logic that ORs 'implemented' flags and concatenates evidence strings per requirement_id
plumb coverage must run pytest --cov and parse output to report line coverage percentage
For spec-to-test coverage, plumb coverage must check whether a test references or maps to each requirement in the cache using string-matching to look for requirement IDs
plumb coverage must scan for link markers in test files to determine coverage
plumb coverage must report count and percentage covered for spec-to-test coverage

For spec-to-code coverage, plumb coverage must use the requirements cache to check whether each requirement has a corresponding implementation by using string-matching to look for requirement IDs in source files
plumb coverage must report gaps in spec-to-code coverage
plumb coverage must print a formatted table using rich
plumb coverage must use LLM calls to refresh the requirements cache for accurate coverage analysis
plumb coverage must measure and report coverage improvement achieved by marker injection

plumb status must print tracked spec files and total requirements, number of tests, pending decisions with branch breakdown if spanning multiple branches, decisions with broken git references, last sync commit, and coverage summary
Plumb must ship with a Claude Code skill file at plumb/skill/SKILL.md
During plumb init, the skill file must be copied to .claude/SKILL.md in the project root as a project-local installation only
The skill must never be installed globally
Claude Code must automatically read files in .claude/ at the start of each session
The skill file must provide structured guidance for AI-assisted development workflow
The skill file must teach Claude Code the Plumb workflow so it can guide the user naturally through the development process
The skill file must provide the machine-readable protocol for parsing hook output and calling per-decision commands during conversational review
The system must support reading Claude Code native session files with JSONL parsing from the ~/.claude/projects/ directory structure
Conversation logs must be located in the real session JSONL files using the format ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-uuid>.jsonl
The .claude/ directory and SKILL.md must be committed to version control so all contributors to the project get the skill automatically
plumb init must append a specific Plumb status block to CLAUDE.md delimited by comment markers so future Plumb commands can update it without affecting surrounding content
If claude_log_path is not set, Plumb must attempt to auto-detect using common Claude Code log locations
If conversation log is not found, Plumb must skip conversation analysis, note 'conversation_available': false in each decision, print a warning, and continue with diff-only analysis
The log must be a JSONL file where each line is a turn with at minimum role, content, and timestamp
Session files must be pre-filtered by modification time to avoid reading old files, then individual entries filtered by timestamp
Plumb must read only turns recorded after the last_commit timestamp in config.json
The conversation parser must handle the native session JSONL format with type/message schema used by Claude Code
Chunking must be performed in conversation.py before any DSPy program is called with no LLM involved
Chunks must be created deterministically
The primary unit must be user turn - a chunk is one user message plus all following assistant turns up to but not including the next user message
If a chunk exceeds 6,000 tokens, it must be split at tool call boundaries
If no tool call boundary exists, chunks must be split at the midpoint of the largest assistant turn
The final assistant turn of the previous chunk must be prepended as a header to the next chunk
One turn of overlap must preserve continuity without meaningfully increasing size
Before chunking, tool result turns longer than 500 tokens whose content appears to be a raw file read must be replaced with '[file read: <filename>]'
Assistant messages that span multiple JSONL entries must be properly parsed and reassembled
DecisionExtractor must be called once per chunk with the diff_summary passed identically to every call
Results must be merged after all chunks with near-duplicate decisions collapsed into one, preserving the earliest chunk_index
The pre-commit hook must fire on amends
To prevent duplicates on amends: compare HEAD's parent SHA to last_commit and if equal, delete decisions where commit_sha == last_commit before re-running analysis
On every hook run, all stored SHAs must be checked against git history
Unreachable SHAs must be flagged 'ref_status': 'broken'
Plumb must not attempt to re-map decisions to new SHAs
The user must review and re-resolve broken-reference decisions manually
The decision log must be append-only with existing lines never modified in place
Status updates must be written as new lines with the same id
The latest line for a given id must be canonical
commit_sha must be null until the commit lands and populated by the hook on the second pass when no pending decisions remain and the commit proceeds
DiffAnalyzer must take raw unified diff string as input and output list of change summaries with files_changed, summary, and change_type
DiffAnalyzer must group related changes into logical units and not invent meaning
DecisionExtractor must take chunk and diff_summary as input and output list of decision objects with question, decision, made_by, related_diff_summary, confidence
DecisionExtractor must extract explicit and implicit decisions and not extract trivial decisions like variable naming or import ordering
QuestionSynthesizer must take a decision object with no associated question as input and output a plain-English question framing the decision for a developer
RequirementParser must take markdown string as input and output list of requirement objects with text and ambiguous fields
RequirementParser must use atomic statements, active voice, no duplicates, and flag vague statements as ambiguous: true and exclude unless user approves
SpecUpdater must take spec_section (markdown) and decision (approved decision object) as input and output updated markdown for that section
SpecUpdater must capture result of decision as natural requirement with no reference to the decision itself and preserve existing formatting
TestGenerator must take requirements (uncovered), existing_tests (file contents), and code_context (relevant source) as input and output runnable pytest test code as a Python string

TestGenerator must use one function per requirement, descriptive names (test_<req_id>_<description>), tests with real assertions against actual code under test, no pytest.skip() or # TODO: implement, and no overwriting existing tests

CodeModifier must use Claude API (not DSPy) and take staged diff, rejected decision, rejection reason, and current spec as input and output modified file contents that satisfy the rejection while remaining consistent with the spec
All CLI commands must fail gracefully with a clear error message if config.json is missing or malformed
All DSPy programs must retry on LLM failure (max 2 retries) then raise PlumbInferenceError with a human-readable message
The git hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
Hook failures must print a warning to stderr and exit 0
File writes (spec updates, test generation) must use temp file → rename to avoid partial writes
If conversation log is unavailable, Plumb must continue with diff-only analysis and this is not an error
If plumb modify test run fails, Plumb must not stage the modification and update decision status to rejected_manual
The plumb status command must operate in cache-only mode to avoid making LLM calls
The plumb coverage command must display progress using an in-place updating spinner, then print the final results table
Session file reading must be optimized by pre-filtering based on file modification times to skip files that cannot contain relevant conversations
Plumb itself must use pytest with 80% coverage minimum for v0.1.0

cli.py tests must verify all commands run without error given valid inputs, per-decision commands update decisions.jsonl correctly, coverage command provides progress feedback with progress bar and status text, and spec and test suggestion functions cover various directory scenarios
decision_log.py tests must verify read/write/filter/dedup on .jsonl and latest-line-wins logic for status updates
git_hook.py tests must verify hook produces correct pending decisions given mock diffs and conversation logs, amend detection, and TTY vs non-TTY output formats
conversation.py tests must verify correct chunk boundaries, overlap, noise reduction, metadata, and oversized chunks split at tool call boundaries

programs/ tests must verify each DSPy program produces correctly structured output given fixture inputs (schema validity, not LLM quality) and comprehensive test suite for chunking functionality covering token estimation, item chunking, and chunked mapper execution scenarios
coverage_reporter.py tests must verify correct calculations given mock pytest output

sync.py tests must verify spec and test files updated correctly given approved decisions, no partial writes, and newline formatting tests covering both apply_section_updates() and insert_new_sections() functions
